mnemonics: Wise Animals Run Swiftly Hungry Lions
Ways Are Really Shortest For You Hiking Locally
keyPoints: Warshall's Algorithm: Transposes the graph to make it easier to find the shortest path between any two vertices.
Floyd's Algorithm: Uses dynamic programming to find the shortest path between any two vertices in a weighted graph.
Both algorithms have a time complexity of O(n^3) and are used for finding the longest path, shortest path, and minimum spanning tree in a graph.
